serialization.policy: Preserve binary64 round-trip precision; solver-specific inputs are derived adapters, not a second authority.

source_exit_surface.policy: Export every qualifying source-exit event; never clip it with a downstream reference distribution.

target_entry_surface.reference_derivation: outer_face_x = accelerator_axis_x - (accelerator_bore_half + accelerator_ring_width + accelerator_insulation_gap + accelerator_shield_wall); y and z use the formal source center
required_before_runtime: parameterized side-port cut through the accelerator shield
physical entry aperture and grounded injection-tube geometry

pulse_capture_state.derivation: Replay the time-stamped entry stream through the connector and collection region to the pulse trigger time.

derived_target_from_source_pose.policy: Derive from the two component poses; never maintain an independently editable duplicate transform.

rigid_transform_rules.time: instrument_time_us is unchanged by a coordinate transform
rigid_transform_rules.physical_policy: A transform registers coordinate frames only; it may not teleport particles across the physical connector gap.

alignment_variables.acceptance_policy: Evaluate alignment errors through the frozen connector and downstream capture model; do not compensate by moving the source distribution in post-processing.

spatial_responsibilities: realize the physical separation and relative angle between source exit and target entry
provide mechanical alignment or physical steering and focusing elements when required
transport particles through the full displaced geometry without coordinate teleportation

target_entry_topology_audit.comsol: accelshield is one outer Block minus one inner Block, leaving continuous x/y side walls and an integrated rear cap; no side-port subtraction exists
target_entry_topology_audit.simion: the grounded accelerator electrode is an outer centered box minus an inner centered box plus a rear closure; no lateral port is removed
target_entry_topology_audit.cad: the COMSOL accelshield Difference is exported as one accelerator_shield solid, so CAD inherits the closed side wall
target_entry_topology_audit.conclusion: The present formal analyzer has no physical +x injection opening. A connector alone outside the shield cannot deliver particles into the release region.

first_gap_geometry.meaning: Zero-clearance geometric ceiling from the current 3.0 mm first accelerator gap; it is not a design value.

coupled_longitudinal_envelope.inequality: physical axial full height must be strictly less than 1.0 mm after applying the design safety factor
derived_from: oa_tof/config/baseline.json geometry_mm accelerator electrode positions and L_stage2
oa_tof/config/baseline.json geometry_derivation.reflectron energy and stage2-margin policy
coupled_longitudinal_envelope.meaning: The fixed coupled oaTOF theory and its 100% stage-2 penetration-depth margin support at most a 1.0 mm source-correlated axial full width. U_R1 and F_2 must come from the coupled total-time solution, never the superseded separately focused reflectron. This is the controlling known bound, not the looser 3.0 mm electrode gap.

ideal_grounded_circular_tube.applicability: L0 dominant Laplace-mode attenuation for a circular grounded tube with normalized boundary-mode coefficient no greater than one; the real side-port field requires 3D validation.

upper_bounds.combined_policy: The usable upper bound is the minimum of the coupled-longitudinal, gap-clearance, shielding/leakage, field-uniformity and high-voltage/breakdown bounds; every applicable bound must be satisfied. Full height and semi-height must never be mixed.

performance_bound.formula: a_required(p) = selected percentile of the propagated target-entry beam envelope + alignment allowance, where p is derived from the frozen minimum geometric transmission
performance_bound.policy: The complete beam support is not a mandatory lower bound. Derive the required percentile from an explicitly approved loss budget, report rejected particles, and never copy the 3.6 mm RF exit radius or the 1 mm oaTOF reference source size.

entry_aperture_design.feasibility_rule: A geometry is theory-admissible when every aperture dimension is positive and strictly below its safety-factored theoretical upper bound. It is performance-eligible only when the measured acceptance of every frozen source case meets the separately frozen minimum transmission. A beam tail outside the aperture is an explicit loss, not proof that no geometry exists.
selection_order: freeze leakage, clearance, high-voltage, field-uniformity and safety requirements
propagate every frozen source case and compute aperture-versus-acceptance tradeoffs
freeze the minimum geometric transmission and derive its percentile envelope
prove both theory admissibility and performance eligibility
select and validate a geometry only inside that interval

constraint_freeze_policy.single_input_authority: oa_tof/config/baseline.json plus this interface contract for connector-only requirements
constraint_freeze_policy.dimension_semantics: Store explicit axial_full_height_mm and axial_semi_height_mm; use diameter_mm only for a frozen circular shape.

required_physics: static or time-dependent magnetic field when present
connector.field_ownership_policy: If upstream and downstream fields overlap, the overlap belongs to one connector or joint model; a one-way particle handoff may not split the overlap.

common_potential_reference.scope: Nominal ideal direct mating only; it does not qualify grounding impedance, finite hardware separation or assembly bonding.

joint_field_ownership.geometry_scope: One connected three-dimensional vacuum domain spanning the RF exit fringe region, coincident shield opening and oa first accelerator gap.
required_field_bases: RF rod differential unit field on the shared S1 geometry
oaTOF always-on electrostatic electrode field on the same shared S1 geometry
joint_field_ownership.superposition_policy: Basis-field superposition is allowed only when every basis is solved on the identical joint geometry, common 0 V reference and compatible discretization; importing a field solved on either closed standalone component is forbidden.
joint_field_ownership.particle_clock_policy: Evaluate the RF waveform from canonical instrument_time_us; a downstream local-time rebase must preserve the identical RF phase.
joint_field_ownership.claim_limit: Ownership is resolved, but no joint field has yet been built, converged or used for particle tracing.

time_control.static_field_policy: A local rebase is allowed only when forces are time independent and the canonical time mapping is retained.
time_control.time_dependent_field_policy: Release each particle at its mapped local time and evaluate every waveform on the same mapped instrument clock.

required_fields: electrode states before and after trigger

target_reference_distribution.policy: The current 1 x 1 x 1 mm formal release distribution is a validation input, not a proven target-entry aperture or pulse-capture acceptance.

storage_policy.canonical: one compact source-exit event row per particle plus one metadata JSON
storage_policy.derived_runtime_inputs: Generate only for the selected downstream solver and run; they do not become parameter authorities.
storage_policy.three_dimensional_snapshot: Generate only when a pulse-capture, space-charge or diagnostic consumer requires it.
storage_policy.dense_trajectories: Diagnostic only; never part of the canonical handoff.
